problem_statement: ReadOnlyWorkerTerminalClosureWitness defines the terminal closure evidence boundary, but worker runtime enablement still needs a schema-backed witness before any runtime worker dispatch can be enabled.
success_criteria: ReadOnlyWorkerRuntimeEnablementWitness validates against a JSON schema and Foundation Mode example
the witness binds the upstream terminal closure witness

the witness requires runtime runner registration, dispatch endpoint registration, receipt emitter registration, receipt-store activation, operator approval, active runtime lease, UAO dispatch authorization, Phi_gov dispatch authorization, runtime dispatch admission, rollback plan, WorkerFailureReceipt obligation on error, and trusted runtime clock evidence

the witness keeps runtime enablement, runtime dispatch admission, runtime dispatch, worker invocation, receipt emission, filesystem writes, connector calls, network access, secret access, and success claims denied
focused tests reject authority drift, contract drift, missing authorization input refs, admission drift, malformed payloads, and summary drift
non_goals: do not perform UAO dispatch authorization
do not emit or append runtime receipts
do not grant filesystem, connector, network, runtime enablement, or success-claim authority
constraints: Foundation Mode remains local-first and witness-only

runtime dispatch remains blocked until terminal closure, runtime registration, operator approval, active lease, UAO authorization, Phi_gov authorization, dispatch admission, rollback, WorkerFailureReceipt, and trusted runtime clock evidence exist
all new artifacts must remain deterministic and read-only
